zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Bellingham於大約1400年出生時，他的父親Richard Bellingham24歲，母親Anne Braburne24歲。他於大約1416年與Elisabeth Tunstall在英國維斯特莫藍德結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1476年3月12日在英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bellingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Bellingham, in Greater London (formerly in Kent) and Northumberland. The former is named with Old English Beringahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the followers of Be(o)ra’, a byname meaning ‘bear’; the latter seems to have been originally named as the ‘homestead of the dwellers at the bell’, from Old English belle used in a transferred sense of a bell-shaped hill.

History: Richard Bellingham (c. 1592–1672) came from Boston, Lincolnshire, England, to Boston, MA, in 1634. He was a controversial political figure in the new colony, an opponent of John Winthrop (see Winthrop ). He was elected governor of MA in 1641 and again in 1654 and 1665–72. — An Irish family of this name trace their descent from William de Bellingham, who was sheriff of Tynedale, Northumbria, in 1279. They were established in Ireland by Robert Bellingham, who settled in County Longford in 1611. They gave their name to Castlebellingham in County Louth, Ireland.

variants.name: Winthrop
meanings: English (Cumberland and Durham): habitational name from any of the places in Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire called Winthorpe. The former is named with the Old English personal name or byname Wine, meaning ‘friend’, + Old Norse thorp ‘settlement’. The Lincolnshire placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Wine + Old Norse thorp ‘secondary settlement, outlying farmstead’. The Nottinghamshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wīgmund or the Old Norse personal name Vígmundr + Old Norse thorp.

History: John Winthrop (1588–1649) was the first governor of the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He kept a detailed journal, an invaluable source for historians. He was born into a gentry family in Suffolk, England, whose fortunes were founded by his grandfather Adam Winthrop (died 1562) of Lavenham. In 1544 the latter acquired a 500-acre estate that had been part of the monastery of Bury St. Edmunds. John Winthrop was a Puritan who emigrated from Groton, Suffolk, to Salem, MA, in 1630 because of Charles I's anti-Puritan policies. By the time of his death he had had four wives and 16 children, the most notable of whom was his son John (1606–76), a scientist and governor of CT. His descendants were prominent in politics and science, including John Winthrop (1714–79), an astronomer, and Robert Winthrop (1809–94), a senator and speaker of the US House of Representatives.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Bellingham was born about 1400, his father, Richard Bellingham, was 24 and his mother, Anne Braburne, was 24. He married Elisabeth Tunstall about 1416, in Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 12 March 1476, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Bellingham est né environ 1400, son père, Richard Bellingham, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anne Braburne, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Elisabeth Tunstall environ 1416, à Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 12 mars 1476, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Bellingham um 1400 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Bellingham 24 und seine Mutter Anne Braburne 24. Er hat um 1416 in Westmorland, England Elisabeth Tunstall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 12. März 1476 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Kendal, Westmorland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Bellingham è nato il circa 1400, il suo padre, Richard Bellingham, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Anne Braburne, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Elisabeth Tunstall circa 1416, nel Westmorland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 12 marzo 1476, nel Kendal, Westmorland, England, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bellinghamが約1400年に生まれた時，父Richard Bellinghamは24歳，母Anne Braburneは24歳であった。約1416年にイギリスウエストモーランドでElisabeth Tunstallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1476年3月12日にイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bellingham(이)가 대략 1400년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Bellingham(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Braburne(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1416년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 Elisabeth Tunstall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1476년 3월 12일 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Bellingham nasceu aproximadamente 1400, seu pai, Richard Bellingham, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Anne Braburne, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Elisabeth Tunstall aproximadamente 1416, em Westmorland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de março de 1476, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Bellingham родился около 1400 г., его отцу по имени Richard Bellingham было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Braburne, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabeth Tunstall около 1416 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 12 марта 1476 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert Bellingham nació aproximadamente 1400, su papá, Richard Bellingham, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Anne Braburne, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Elisabeth Tunstall aproximadamente 1416, en Westmorland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 12 de marzo de 1476, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años.
